title: Red Hat Security Advisory: buildah security update
overview:
An update for buildah is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.6 Extended Update Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

affected_products: Red Hat Enterprise Linux AppStream EUS (v.9.6)
full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: buildah security update

An update for buildah is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.6 Extended Update Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for buildah is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.6 Extended Update Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

Updated Packages:
buildah-1.39.6-1.el9_6.src.rpm
buildah-1.39.6-1.el9_6.x86_64.rpm
buildah-debuginfo-1.39.6-1.el9_6.x86_64.rpm

buildah-tests-1.39.6-1.el9_6.s390x.rpm
buildah-tests-debuginfo-1.39.6-1.el9_6.s390x.rpm
buildah-1.39.6-1.el9_6.ppc64le.rpm
buildah-debuginfo-1.39.6-1.el9_6.ppc64le.rpm
buildah-debugsource-1.39.6-1.el9_6.ppc64le.rpm
buildah-tests-1.39.6-1.el9_6.ppc64le.rpm
buildah-tests-debuginfo-1.39.6-1.el9_6.ppc64le.rpm
buildah-1.39.6-1.el9_6.aarch64.rpm
buildah-debuginfo-1.39.6-1.el9_6.aarch64.rpm
buildah-debugsource-1.39.6-1.el9_6.aarch64.rpm
buildah-tests-1.39.6-1.el9_6.aarch64.rpm
buildah-tests-debuginfo-1.39.6-1.el9_6.aarch64.rpm

CVE-2025-58183: A flaw was found in the archive/tar package in the Go standard library. tar.Reader does not set a maximum size on the number of sparse region data blocks in GNU tar pax 1.0 sparse files. A specially crafted tar archive with a pax header indicating a big number of sparse regions can cause a Go program to try to allocate a large amount of memory, causing an out-of-memory condition and resulting in a denial of service.
